title: Evangelion Media
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Media.             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Sat Aug 22, 2009 8:48 pm I am trying to complete my NGE collection. I currently have the collectors edition of Neon Genesis Evangelion, End of Evangelion, volumes 1-11 of the NGE manga, volumes 1-6 of Angelic days, and 1 of The raising Shinji Ikari Project. Should I buy Death and Rebirth, or is it not necessary. I never understood what EXACTLY is in Death and Rebirth. Last time I watched it was like 5 years ago and I can't remember what happened in it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Aug 22, 2009 9:19 pm Death & Rebirth is basically the TV series squashed to 60 minutes, with the first half of End of Evangelion tacked on after an intermission. But there is some new animation on D&R. Some of the animation from the TV series is changed shot-wise and/or cleaned up some. And there's some new shots of Shinji, Asuka, Rei, and Kaworu practicing then playing instruments for a string quartet. They way they use some creative editing to tell each child's story by introducing their instrument is interesting, and I bought it simply because of that. Sometimes you even see the events of the TV series in a different point of view simply because Asuka's violin is playing over the scene rather than the sound of Shinji's tape player that was originally in the scene. So it's almost like you're seeing that scene through Asuka's perspective rather than Shinji's. I bought it for those musical reason listed above.

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Sat Aug 22, 2009 10:04 pm Well you helped me make up my mind. I will most definitely pick up Death and Rebirth now. Thanks a million FreakyFilmFan4ever!

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:04 am Try to find the Japanese laserdisc releases of the Series (the Genesis Discs) if not for watching, just for the awesome cover art.

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Sun Aug 23, 2009 10:07 am scarmullet wrote: Try to find the Japanese laserdisc releases of the Series (the Genesis Discs) if not for watching, just for the awesome cover art.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Aug 23, 2009 10:21 am NeverKnowsBest wrote: Death and Rebirth

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:23 am NeverKnowsBest wrote: scarmullet wrote: Try to find the Japanese laserdisc releases of the Series (the Genesis Discs) if not for watching, just for the awesome cover art.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:31 am Also, you should think about picking up this: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_notenki_memoirs.php DO NOT BUY THIS: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_unofficial_guide.php It sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:46 am I watched Death & Rebirth once and I haven't watched it since. ^^; I definitely like having it in my collection, but I find I'm fully satisfied by the TV series with director's cut episodes, and the End of Evangelion. Monkey-sama wrote: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_notenki_memoirs.php

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:49 am Do they have Puchi Eva @ School on Dvd?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:58 am scarmullet wrote: COVERS there are 14 discs and then the theatrical box set. Eva series Japanese LD cover art Films

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:50 pm LiLi wrote: scarmullet wrote: COVERS there are 14 discs and then the theatrical box set. Eva series Japanese LD cover art Films

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 23, 2009 2:58 pm ^ Yeah, I was under the impression DVDs had become more popular/common than LDs more due to merchandising issues rather than quality issues? I don't own a LD player unfortunately, but as far as DVDs go, I'd say the PQ depends on the individual release, really... My own Eva Boxset is very... essential but I do think there was a previous one with lots of goodies... I was wondering if those nice LDs have the pre-Renewal version of Eva though?

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Sun Aug 23, 2009 3:07 pm LiLi wrote: ^ Yeah, I was under the impression DVDs had become more popular/common than LDs more due to merchandising issues rather than quality issues? I don't own a LD player unfortunately, but as far as DVDs go, I'd say the PQ depends on the individual release, really... My own Eva Boxset is very... essential but I do think there was a previous one with lots of goodies... I was wondering if those nice LDs have the pre-Renewal version of Eva though?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 23, 2009 3:55 pm The series LDs released the DC episodes (no on-air episodes) as well as the "video version" episodes of EoE which have various changes from the film version: http://www.angelfire.com/anime4/mdwigs/Movies.html The End of Evangelion: Theatrical release Vs. Video release The End of Evangelion was released in video in two parts in Japan. "Genesis 0:13" featured episode 25 and episode 25' and "Genesis 0:14" featured episode 26 and episode 26'. For their Video release the episode contained within "The End of Evangelion" were altered slightly to be more in form with the video style of episode. Below is a list of the changes between the Video version of each episode and the Theatrical Version. The Video Version of Episode 25': Eye catch: "Neon Genesis Evangelion Episode: 25' Love is destructive." Credits for just episode 25 are shown at the end to the song "Thanatos - If I Can't Be Yours - (Short Version)" At the end of the episode there is a preview of the next episode. The Video Version of Episode 26': Title screen: "Neon Genesis Evangelion" In the kitchen scene Asuka's "Iya" (No) is written on the screen, we don't hear it. Eye catch: "Neon Genesis Evangelion One More Final: I need you." The caption at the conclusion of the episode says simply "End" Credits for just episode 26' are shown at the end to a string version of "Jesus bleibet meine Freude" The Theatrical Version of Episode 25': Eye catch: "The End of Evangelion Episode:25' Love is destructive." Credits for both episodes 25' and 26' are shown at the end of episode 25 to the song "Thanatos - If I Can't Be Yours" There is no preview of the next episode. The Theatrical Version of Episode 26': Title screen: "The End of Evangelion" In the kitchen scene Asuka's "Iya" (liar) is said and not written on the screen. Eye catch: "The End of Evangelion One More Final: I need you." The caption at the conclusion of the episode says "End of the movie" There are no ending credits, just a black screen.

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Sun Aug 23, 2009 5:05 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: NeverKnowsBest wrote: Death and Rebirth

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 23, 2009 5:11 pm ^Are you only interested in local US releases/subbed releases? (Just checking, since I can't help with that...)

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Sun Aug 23, 2009 11:04 pm ^ Yeah. Like I am from the states and I would assume that is what I am looking for. I just want the best possible experience with death and rebirth and not feel cheated at not having the best possible DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Mon Aug 24, 2009 1:02 am There isn't much to expect with death and rebirth. Its just best to get it for completeness I think I payed a buck for my DVD at a Rummage sale.

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Mon Aug 24, 2009 5:53 am scarmullet wrote: There isn't much to expect with death and rebirth. Its just best to get it for completeness I think I payed a buck for my DVD at a Rummage sale.
